title: Port an assembly routine across architectures
description: Move a hand-asm routine between ISAs/ABIs by remapping the calling convention, registers, and addressing, validated against the original.
body: Steps: 1. Identify the source and target ABI differences: argument/return registers, callee-saved sets, stack alignment, and red-zone/shadow-space rules. 2. Remap each argument and the return value to the target ABI's locations. 3. Translate instructions, addressing modes, and operand widths to the target ISA (x86-64 rich addressing vs AArch64 register/offset forms). 4. Rebuild prologue/epilogue for the target's callee-saved registers and alignment. 5. Validate the ported routine against the original (or a C reference) across many inputs on the target. Rollback point: keep the portable high-level fallback so the port is optional. Validation: the ported routine matches the reference across the input range on the target architecture and the disassembly shows correct register and alignment discipline; assuming the register mapping carries over unchanged is how ports silently corrupt arguments.
